title: apocynum androsaemifolium var. tomentellum
intro: Apocynum androsaemifolium var. tomentellum, also known as woolly dogbane, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to North America. Traditionally, woolly dogbane has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating various skin conditions, including eczema and acne. Woolly dogbane is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
